observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor gives utmost importance on
presenting in the financial statements’ PPE account balances that are valid
and reliablebyrequiringtheInventoryCommitteeand theGSO to:
a. Conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s assets and
onlyincludethosepropertiesactuallycounted;
b. Submit the IIRUP for the disposed properties and make the necessary
adjustments; and
c. Prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to the Audit
Team at least 10 days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking
activities in adopting COA Circular 2020-006 relative to the one-time
cleansingof PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to prepare and
submit theIIRUP and to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the
saidpropertiesinaccordancewithapplicablerulesand regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to renew the PAR
every January of the third year after its issuance or whenever there is a
change in the custodianship. Henceforth, comply strictly with Section 492 of
theGAAM, Volume1 toensuretheaccountabilityof thepropertyissued.

We also recommended that all PPE items be tagged with property stickers
containing the property number and other vital information for easy
identification.

WerecommendedthattheMunicipal Mayor directtheMunicipal Accountant to
verify with the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of P11,828.72 and
P222,933.61 between the recorded balances of the reciprocal accounts
reflected in the books of the Province and adjust the same to present the
correctbalancesof thesaidaccountsintheMunicipality’sfinancial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor include in the proposed budget
theamountrequired forthepurchaseof landand utilizethefundsintendedfor
theconstructionof theGumacaMunicipal Nursery.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to compile the photographs of the aforementioned recipients during the actual payout with the signed payrolls and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents such as the signed payrolls will be supported with the SACs, actual IDs, photographs and names of the recipients, cellphone numbers of the recipients, if any, and submitted to the Commission on Audit on July 23, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO to submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances together with the required supporting documents to the NDRRMC through the Office of the Civil Defense and furnish the Audit Team within 10 working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009 in order for the Audit Team and the oversight agencies to conduct timely evaluation of the receipt and utilization of the aids and donations received by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already submitted the required list.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to review the Cash in Bank - LCCA account and record the funds pertaining to the Gumaca Institutional Microfinance Assistance in the books of the Municipality under the Trust Fund account to fairly present the financial statements; and b. Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cash book for the bank account no. 2442-1077-96 (Gumaca Institutional Microfinance Assistance).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already recorded in the CIB account the funds pertaining to the Gumaca Institutional Microfinance Assistance in the books of the Municipality under the Trust Fund account. The Municipal Treasurer has already maintained a separate cashbook for the Gumaca Institutional Microfinance Assistance account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the: •Municipal Assessor to submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Assessment Roll; •Municipal Treasurer to submit the list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts;and •Municipal Accountant to accrue or establish the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: Updated Assessment Roll was already submitted. The Municipal Treasurer submitted the list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible at the start of each year. The Municipal Accountant had set-up RPT/SET Receivables on June10, 2021.
observations.reason: Late set-up was made due to late submission of Certified List of Taxpayer by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the a.Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to conduct a complete physical count of the PPE owned by the Municipality at year-end and prepare a completely filled-out the report and the RPCPPE; and conduct the physical count; and b.Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to exert extra effort in retrieving documents for the reconciling and unaccounted/unknown properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO conducted a complete physical count of the PPE owned by the Municipality during the year. The Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to exert extra effort in retrieving documents for the unaccounted/unknown properties of the Municipality. The GSO started reconciling and retrieve report.
observations.reason:
The submitted RPCPPE includes properties that were remarked as unaccounted and disposed of, thus making the reliability of the conduct of the physical count questionable.
Still on going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to: a.coordinate with the Land Registration Authority/Registry of Deeds to secure a copy of the list of lands belonging to the Municipality, if possible, then trace whether the land listed were integrated in the unaccounted balance and identify which parcels of land were not supported with TCT; and b.facilitate the immediate titling of lots in the name of the Municipality and continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Coordinate with the LRA and RD regarding titling of untitled lots. Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer are continuously exerting efforts to facilitate the titling of the lots.
observations.reason: On-going coordination with the LRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist to submit on time to the Municipal Accountant the monitoring report stating the condition and the fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2022-06-100147 dated June 6, 2022 was submitted to reflect the correct balance of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in retrieving documents for the existing dormant payables. If not avail, revert the P182,181.13 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claims arises in the future pursuant to the provision of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Accountant has already sent a letter to the Municipal Mayor to file the request for the write-off through the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDO and Municipal Engineer to: •submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year; •inform the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices were already posted for validation; •submit the monthly monitoring report as current as possible and updated; •ensure the inclusion of all necessary information on project signboards in compliance with the above-cited circular;and •stop the charging of additional tarpaulin or project signage which information can already be found on the tarpaulin required by the above-cited circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to update and submit the list of all on-going as well as projects for implementation. The Management was able to provide notice of award and pictures of the signboards of the projects. The Municipal Engineer was able to submit and update the monthly monitoring report. The Management updated their project signboards and indicated the necessary information. The Management discontinued the charging of additional tarpaulin/project signage.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MAO and the Head of Municipal PESO to exert effort for the immediate utilization of the funds received from the National Government Agencies to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized funds from DOLE of P785,230.00 and DA of P2,963,978.00 were either returned or utilized by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and HRMO to coordinate with the GSIS for the updates of its database and the Municipal Treasurer to effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions. We also recommend that they reconcile the account to enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits due them and to establish the accuracy of the account balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Coordination was made with the GSIS Lucena, continuous tracing and retrieval of records were made and any reconciled records/ accounts were remitted or adjusted accordingly.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation/continuous tracing and retrieval of records; any reconciled records/accounts were remitted or adjusted accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly Certified List of Taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances; and Municipal Accountant to accrue or establish the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in audit observation no. 4 page36.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to submit the Certified List of Taxpayers because the Municipal Assessor still fails to submit the updated roll of assessment as it was not given priority, thus the Municipal Accountant was not able to set-up the RPT/SET receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to compile the aforementioned documents wherein the signed payrolls will be supported with the SACs, IDs, photographs with names of the recipients, cellphone numbers of the recipients,if any, andother supportingdocuments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances together with the required supporting documents to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnish the Audit Team within 10 working days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-009 in order for the Audit Team and the oversight agencies to conduct timely evaluation of the receipt and utilization of the aids and donations received bytheMunicipality.

WerecommendedthattheMunicipal Mayor requirethe:
a. Municipal Accountant to review the Cash in Bank - LCCA account and record the funds pertaining to the Gumaca Institutional Microfinance Assistance in the books of the Municipality under the Trust Fund account to fairly present the financialstatements; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cash book for the bank account no. 2442-1077-96(GumacaInstitutional MicrofinanceAssistance).

 Municipal Assessor to submit to the Municipal Treasurer the updated Assessment Roll;
 Municipal Treasurer to submit the list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables in order toreflecta reliableRPT and SETReceivable accountsbalances; and
 Municipal Accountant to accrue or establish the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning oftheyear.

b. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to exert extra effort in retrieving documentsfor theunaccounted/unknownpropertiesof theMunicipality

b. facilitate the immediate titling of lots in the name of the Municipality and continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit on time to the Municipal Accountant the monitoring report stating the conditionandthefair value lesscost tosellof thebiologicalassetsas of year-end of CY 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort inretrieving documents for the existing dormant payables. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDO and Municipal
Engineer to:
 submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be
implemented during the year to the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the
year;
 inform the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or
before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account/s and ensure that deductions/credits to these accounts are based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) prepared and submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, require her to validate and update records relative to the issued supplies and materials, and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements and promote better control of its receiptsandissuances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has recorded the supplies and materials purchased in the inventory account. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor gives utmost importance on presenting in the financial statements PPE account balances that are valid and reliable by requiring the: a.Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s assets and prepare and submit the required report thereon. Investigate any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed;and b.Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and updated property cards, as a counter check to the correctness of the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted a physical count of property; Complete and updated property cards were maintained by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The Municipality conducted a complete physical count of PPE. The Municipality was not able to submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to: a.coordinate with the Land Registration Authority (LRA)/Registry of Deeds to secure a copy of the list of lands belonging to the Municipality, if possible, then trace whether the land listed were integrated in the said unaccounted balance and identify which parcels of land were not supported with TCT; and b.facilitate the immediate titling of lots in the name of the Municipality and continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor commented that the coordination with the Registry of Deeds with regard to the list of lands belonging to the Municipality has been started, however, it was delayed due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Moreover, the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer mentioned that the immediate titling of lots in the name of the Municipality were currently on-going.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to coordinate with the LRA due to the restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exerted effort in retrieving documents that would facilitate the immediate titling of the subject lots. The only documents that the Municipal Accountant retrieved was a ledger that indicate details like General Public Services, Education, Culture and Sports Development and the Transfer from TF for Land Inventory; however, as to the information needed to facilitate the titling was still missing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to submit to the Municipal Accountant a monitoring report stating the condition and the fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets as of year-end of CY 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist belatedly submitted the inventory report. The Municipal Accountant was not able to update the valuation of the biological assets.
observations.reason: Due to the late submission by the Municipal Agriculturist the Municipal Accountant was not able to update the valuation of the biological assets. Moreover, the submitted monitoring report is doubtful due to discrepancies in the age and years recorded.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with the account owners/suppliers and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims. If they failed to do so within a reasonable period of time after they were notified, revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the SB through a resolution, with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claims arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The unclaimed payables were already reverted back to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in locating the documents for possible collection. If to no avail, we recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed with the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The request for write-off was filed with Audit Team and currently under review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the P5,954.03 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claims arises in the future pursuant to the provision of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already requested for the write-off of Due to Other GOCCs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: Insurable properties of the Municipality were already insured as of March 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO, MAO and the Head of Municipal PESO to exert effort for the immediate utilization of the funds received from National Government Agencies to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail the maximum benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to utilize P586,432.00 for the DSWD Sustainable Livelihood Program. The Municipality was able to implement various projects for the remaining P5,249,208.00; however, implementation of some projects were not carried out by the Municipality due to the following reasons; (1) awaiting approval of the DSWD for the extension of the implementation of the project, (2) revision of the prices of items to be procured and (3) lack of information on the purpose of the funds received.
observations.reason: The Municipality was able to utilize part of the funds; however, due to awaiting DSWD approval for extension, price revisions, and lack of information on fund purpose, remaining funds were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRM Officer to maximize the use of available resources on the LDRRMF for disaster mitigation and preparedness in order to build the resilience of the Municipality against disasters.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer closely monitored the implementation of the PPAs under LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO was able to comply with the monthly submission of the report within the required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to coordinate with the GSIS for the updates of its database and the Municipal Treasurer to effect timely and accurate remittance of GSIS premiums, loans and contributions. We also recommended that they reconcile the account to enable the member beneficiaries of the Municipality to avail the benefits due them and to establish the accuracy of the account balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO, Municipal Accountant and Liaison Officer commented that they are still in the process of reconciling the records to facilitate the remittance of unremitted contributions. Out of the unremitted balance amounting to P480,132.01, P141,091.36 was remitted and refunded, leaving an unremitted balance of P339,040.65. Any reconciled records will be remitted immediately.
observations.reason: Due to differences in the Accounting records and GSIS database; reconciliation and updates in the GSIS database are still in progress.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to submit to the Municipal Accountant a
duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due
and collectible at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real
Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables in order to reflect a reliable RPT
and SET Receivable accounts balances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate
inventory account/s and ensure that deductions/credits to these accounts are
based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) prepared and
submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, require her to validate and
update records relative to the issued supplies and materials, and make the
necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the expense and
inventory accounts in the financial statements and promote better control of its
receipts and issuances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor gives utmost importance on presenting
in the financial statements PPE account balances that are valid and reliable by
requiring the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s assets and prepare and submit the required report thereon.
Investigate any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting
and property records and make the necessary adjustments where the error was
committed; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and updated property cards, as a
counter check to the correctness of the accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and
the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. coordinate with the Land Registration Authority/Registry of Deeds to secure a copy of
the list of lands belonging to the Municipality, if possible, then trace whether the land
listed were integrated in the said unaccounted balance and identify which parcels of
land were not supported with TCT; and
b. facilitate the immediate titling of lots in the name of the Municipality and
continuously coordinate with other government agencies to avoid contingent
claims that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to
submit to the Municipal Accountant a monitoring report stating the condition and
the fair value less cost to sell of the biological assets as of year-end of CY 2019.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to confirm with the account owners/suppliers and to request them to
submit documents pertaining to their claims. If they failed to do so within a
reasonable period of time after they were notified, revert the accounts to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan
(SB) through a resolution, with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event
that claims arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
exert more effort in locating the documents for possible collection. If to no avail,
we recommended that request for write-off of receivables be filed with the
Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
revert the P5,954.03 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an
authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that
appropriation shall be made in the event that claims arises in the future pursuant to the
provision of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to
ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with
appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA
Circular No. 92-30 and RA No. 656.

We reiterated our recommendation that the designated GSO to submit the complete RPCPPE pursuant to the MNGAS Volume I using the form prescribed therein and the same must be certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Local Chief Executive.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted physical count of its PPE and submitted the RPCPPE.
observations.reason:
The RPCPPE submitted was still incomplete and unreconciled with the FS balances prior to their term and non-inclusion of unserviceable properties were not yet dropped in the book.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 3, page 32.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account/s and ensure that deductions/credits to these accounts are based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) prepared and submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, require her to validate and update records relative to the issued office supplies and other office supplies and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated in the Summary of Audit Observation and Recommendation (SAOR) that beginning CY 2020, all purchases will be recorded under the appropriate supplies inventory account. All consumed or used supplies will be recorded in the SSMI which will be submitted to the Accounting Office for recording to properly reflect the inventory of supplies and materials.
observations.reason:
Supplies and Materials for each department was prepared.
Supplies and Materials were not recorded in its appropriate inventory account because recording of such was overlooked.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims. If they failed to do so within a reasonable period of time after they were notified, revert the accounts to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claims arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Muncipality have traced the outstanding payables among them three were cancelled. They have also made a request to the SB for the reversion to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.reason: Outstanding payables remained unreverted to the unappropriated surplus due to the lack of SB Resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the submission of documents pertaining to the claims of the account owners/creditors. If they are unable to support their claims with the necessary documents, revert the account balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Muncipality have traced the outstanding guaranty deposit payable for one of the contractor since it claimed mobilization fee. They have adjusted the guaranty deposit payable and will request reversion to the unappropriated surplus subject to an authority from the SB.
observations.reason: Outstanding guaranty deposit payables which contractors can no longer be located will be reverted to the unappropriated surplus subject to an authority from the SB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated General Services Officer to immediately cause the registration of the said motor vehicles and to closely monitor its yearly registration in order to avoid payment of penalty for late registration and to protect the Municipality’s interest over its assets.
observations.managementAction: The Management insured and registered three of the motor vehicles. They have also inspected the nine motor vehicles and they were found to be unserviceable, hence no registration at LTO was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GFPS use the GAD Plan and Budget in the formulation of the GAD AR and to attach the documents required in Paragraph 5 of Section 4.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the GAD programs and projects implemented during the year.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focval Point System used the GAD Plan and Budget in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.reason: GAD Focval Point System used the GAD Plan and AR Budget in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to revert back the unutilized LDRRMF of CY 2013 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reverted back the LDRRMF of CY 2013 to the unappropriated surplus under SB Resolution on August 23, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the designated General Services Officer (GSO) to furnish the Audit Team and Municipal Accountant copy of the complete inventory reports. We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant and designated GSO to maintain subsidiary records and property cards respectively, and ensure that both records are reconciled.
observations.reason: The physical count made by the Municipality was incomplete and the reconciliation of the discrepancies has not yet been made.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
designated GSO to submit the complete RPCPPE pursuant to the MNGAS Volume
I using the form prescribed therein and the same must be certified correct by the
Inventory Committee and approved by the Local Chief Executive.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
record all purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory
account/s and ensure that deductions/credits to these accounts are based on the
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) prepared and submitted by the
Municipal Treasurer’s Office. Likewise, require her to validate and update records
relative to the issued office supplies and other office supplies and make the
necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the expense and
inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to
request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality.
In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall
be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of
Section 98 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
require the submission of documents pertaining to the claims of the account
owners/creditors. If they are unable to support their claims with the necessary
documents, revert the account balances to the Unappropriated Surplus of the
General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a
Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that
claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated General
Services Officer to immediately cause the registration of the said motor vehicles
and to closely monitor its yearly registration in order to avoid payment of penalty
for late registration and to protect the Municipality’s interest over its assets.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the report at the Office of the Municipal Mayor and an Executive Order Memorandum entitled “Municipal Appraisal and Disposal Committee” was issued to create a Disposal Team on October 8, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Other Receivables account.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made by Municipal Accountant. In addition, confirmation letters were sent to validate and identify accountabilities of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P597,836.16 from the now-dissolved agency Presidential Committee on Flagship Programs Projects was remitted to the Bureau of Treasury. Other accounts were adjusted due to erroneous recording.
observations.reason: The remaining accounts were not confirmed with their respective account owners due to the unknown mailing addresses.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least one percent of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality appropriated was able to appropriate at least one percent of their IRA for LCPC for CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to submit to the Office of the Auditor the copies of purchase orders and their supporting documents within five working days from issuance, and inform the deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 96-010, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor copies of purchase orders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Treasurer updated the insurances of all the insurable properties with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
designated GSO to furnish the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant copy
of the complete inventory reports.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant and
designated GSO to maintain subsidiary records and property cards respectively,
and ensure that both records are reconciled.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the GSO to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties
in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer (o reconcile their records and make the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Other Receivables
account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors/employees and to
request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the Municipality.
In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation
shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future pursuant to the
provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC as
required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer/General Services Officer to submit to the Office of the Auditor the
copics of purchasc orders and their supporting documents within five (5)
working days from issuance, and inform the deliveries within twenty-four (24)
hours from acceptance in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and
COA Circular No 96-010, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal
Treasurer see to it that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are
covered with insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provision of COA
Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to file the necessary request for relief from accountability with COA and submit the Livestock Death Certificate, if any, to support the said request for relief, otherwise, require the beneficiaries to refund the cost of the animals.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist conducted further investigation in order to gather necessary documents.
observations.reason:
Difficulty in locating the recipients of breeding stocks as well as the records relative thereto.

Reiterated in Part II of this report, Finding No. 3, pages 39 - 40

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to remit funds to the National Treasury in accordance with Executive Order No. 431 dated May 30, 2005.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to implement this on 2016, however an amount was partially remitted in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes which provide benefits to its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality committed to implement the 20% development fund as early as first quarter.
observations.reason:
The Municipality received funds from various National Government Agencies such as the Department of Health, Department of Agriculture, Department of Energy, and the Department of Social Welfare and Development, hence these funds were utilized and implemented first.

Reiterated in Part II of this report, Finding No. 7, pages 46 - 48

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1.	designated General Services Officer (GSO) to furnish the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant copy of the complete inventory reports; and
2.	Municipal Accountant and designated GSO to maintain subsidiary records and property cards, respectively, and ensure that both records are reconciled.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit timely Livestock Death Certificate to the Municipal Accountant to support the dropping from the books the cost of dead animals in accordance with the rules and regulations on the request for relief from accountability and conduct a more intensive monitoring and evaluation of the project implementation to determine the success or failure of the program for further decision making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the concerned Municipal Officials responsible for the lost properties as evidenced by the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) and other supporting documents submit the above documents as vital requirements for the request for relief from accountability in compliance with the above cited provisions of law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send collection notices and adopt efficient and aggressive collection strategies to enforce collection of the amount of P893,587.77 from concerned beneficiaries. If all means failed, resort to legal action in accordance with the provisions of the Acknowledgement Receipt and Promissory Note under existing laws and regulations to avoid possible loss of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) to speed up the implementation of the above listed projects for the benefits of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
utilize fully the 20% Development Fund to ensure that the intended beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that may be derived by observing the timeline of activities involved in the procurement process thru proper planning to ensure that projects programmed for the year are implemented; and
instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to fast tract the implementation of all infrastructure projects funded from the 20% Development Fund, as well as projects funded by National Government Agencies for the benefit of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, take into consideration the following as control and monitoring measures in establishing internal policies that would promote prudence in the provision for prepaid load/cellular allowance:
consider the use of post-paid plan offered by telecommunication networks with monthly billing;
utilize alternative modes of communication such as internet for long-distance communication;
consider communication network offering free calls between and among networks; and
adopt and implement control measures by limiting the grant to not lower than the Division Chief rank and the amounts of entitlement and any excess on the limits set for amounts shall be for the personal account of the user.
If cell cards shall be allowed, consider purchase in bulk by the GSO, following the required procurement procedures and distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines.
Require the refund of the P1,000.00 and P1,500.00 granted to each regular/casual employee on a monthly basis or in the total amount of P232,500.00 in CY 2016 which fall under the category of excessive expenditures as disclosed in the cases that are considered "Excessive" Expenditures of Government Funds under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the reconciled report of fully-depreciated unserviceable properties.
observations.reason:
The Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property was submitted to the Audit Team, however, necessary actions for disposal of said equipment were not undertaken.

Reiterated in Part II of this report, Finding No. 2, pages 38 - 39

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the prizes received as prior year’s income by debiting Other Payables account and crediting Prior Period Adjustments account, consequently close the Prior Period Adjustments to Government Equity account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments on the books and transferred the same to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board Officials strictly observe the provisions of DepEd Order No. 12, series of 2014, Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and other rules and regulations, relating to the defined usage or utilization of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements were paid in proper charges upon receipt of the recommendation pertaining to the expenses incurred in utilities and communication.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor for evaluation all the required documents in accordance with PD No. 1445 and COA-DBM-DILG-GCC-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required documents to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit required data to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period with the end view of generating timely and relevant audit results.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now religiously complying relative to the submission of required data to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
